utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format has 5 top-level items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final coverage plan includes all 5 top-level items. Now check if each is fully specified with all details as required: each item needs the specified type of insurance and costs. 1) health_coverage: includes physical and mental health insurance with costs — provided with multiple subplans and costs; satisfies. 2) property_coverage: requires home/contents insurance with costs — provided (home_content and tech protection) with costs; satisfies. 3) travel_coverage: requires travel insurance with costs — provided with multiple subplans and costs; satisfies. 4) additional_services: requires any enhancement services with costs — provided as an empty object, so not fully specified. 5) total_budget: complete monthly/annual cost breakdown — provided as a monthly total only; partial but still a cost breakdown; likely acceptable as a 'complete monthly' breakdown (though annual not given). Therefore, 4 items are fully specified (health, property, travel, total_budget), and 1 item (additional_services) is not.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: Extract plan names from the final coverage plan and match them exactly to entries in the ground-truth ratings. The final plan includes: EliteCare Premium Plan (physical health), Preventive Care Package Add-on (corresponds to Preventive Care Package under health_enhancement_services), PrimeCare Premium Plan (mental health), EliteHome Premium Coverage (home content), Technology Protection Add-on (corresponds to Technology Protection under property_enhancement_services), EliteTravel Premium Plan (travel), Adventure Travel Coverage Add-on (corresponds to Adventure Travel Coverage under travel_enhancement_services), Business Travel Support Add-on (corresponds to Business Travel Support), and 24/7 Travel Assistance. For each, retrieve the exact rating number from the ground truth.
